description:
Goal
Repair value-projection fallbacks that currently produce `any` when the compiler lacks precise field/key/pinned evidence. The right answer is a source fact, obligation, explicit dynamic boundary, or diagnostic, not silent top.

Signal
- Map index, field access, required map value, dispatch map/bitstring projections, missing semantic value lookup, and pinned comparison fallbacks are either precise, explicitly dynamic, or ticketed as a blocking data-model gap.
- Tests distinguish unknown dynamic keys from known-missing keys and from pending schema/field obligations.
- Struct field/schema interactions are reconciled with `fz-rh2.17.5.6.8`; if that ticket blocks a cleanup here, wire the dependency instead of encoding `any` locally.
- No backend/display/planner patch hides a poisoned projection result downstream.

Tactics
- Trace `map_field_lookup(...).unwrap_or_else(any_ty)`, `value_fact(... any_ty)`, `ProjectionKind::MapValue/BitstringField => any`, and `ComparisonValue::Pinned(_) => any` to their owning facts.
- Prefer adding or consuming precise obligations over widening values locally.
- When the source language truly permits dynamic unknown, name that boundary and test it.

Outro
No silent field/key/pinned fallback to `any`, no local projection shim, no dropped missing-field diagnostic, and no unresolved struct/schema issue left unticketed or unblocked.
title: Compiler2 value flow: repair projection top fallbacks
